title: VK_GLOBAL_OBJ link rule mistake gives undefined symbol: _ZN16VerilatedContextD1Ev
body:
Sorry if this is a dumb question. 

I'm trying to migrate from 4.102 to 4.202. I have read that VerilatedContext is a new feature in 4.2 

My existing 4.102 flow is to create a Verilated model of my core using --protect-lib, and include this in a VCS simulation. This is the only Verilated model in my environment, so I assume I need only a default singular VerilatedContext somewhere.

When I compile the model with 4.202 (the most recent version I am able to compile on my ancient CentOS7 crate) I see the above error when I run the executable.

./simv: symbol lookup error: verilator/libverilated_thing.so: undefined symbol: _ZN16VerilatedContextD1Ev

The documentation mentions a default context being created by default, but I image this is not practical for a protect-lib model, so my question is, what do I need to add to my simulation executable to expose this symbol in a usable way?

Do I just need to export a function called VerilatedContext which returns a pointer to a lump of memory my model can use to store its state?

Apologies if this is already documented, but I couldn't find it.
